name: Weinstein Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The phase space of an integrable Hamiltonian system, closed under canonical transformations and bounded by the action integrals

VINIT.justification: The void state before perturbation — a completely integrable Hamiltonian H₀ with no external disturbance, the genesis of the theorem's domain

TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor that contains and bounds the entire system — the set of surviving invariant tori that persists under small perturbations, the hermetic seal of the theorem

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism toward the perturbed regime — the directed transformation from integrable to nearly-integrable dynamics, growth of complexity

AREV.justification: Reverse descent — the backward morphism that recovers the unperturbed action variables as ε → 0, the coagula that reconstitutes the original structure

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining of action-angle transformations and near-identity maps that build the full KAM proof structure

IMSCRIB.justification: Identity / self-reference — the action integrals are self-recognizing invariants that identify themselves under the canonical transformation, the stone that knows itself

FSPLIT.justification: Splits the phase space into two branches: resonant tori (where ω·k = 0 for some k) and non-resonant tori (Diophantine condition |ω·k| > γ|k|⁻τ)

FFUSE.chosen_element: KAM theorem conclusion: non-resonant tori persist
FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the full phase space structure by proving that sufficiently non-resonant tori survive perturbation, fusing the two branches back into a single perturbed system where most tori remain invariant

EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch — the positive evaluation that a torus is sufficiently non-resonant and therefore survives the perturbation

EVALF.chosen_element: resonance condition met: ω·k = 0 for some k
EVALF.justification: Negative branch — the failure condition where a torus is resonant and may be destroyed or chaotically perturbed

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the boundary layer near resonance: ε-dependent survival threshold
ENGAGR.justification: Paradice held without resolution — tori near the resonance boundary exist in a superposition of survival and destruction, held simultaneously in the albedo stage where perturbation strength ε determines their fate

IFIX.chosen_element: the measure-theoretic record: Lebesgue measure of surviving tori
IFIX.justification: Permanent irreversible record — the KAM theorem fixes the fact that the complement of surviving tori has measure zero, an append-only determinative that cannot be undone

phase_2.split_element: resonance dichotomy: resonant vs. non-resonant tori
phase_2.split_input: the perturbed phase space with Hamiltonian H = H₀ + εH₁
split_outputs: resonant tori (ω·k = 0 for some k ∈ ℤⁿ, k ≠ 0) — destroyed or chaotically perturbed
non-resonant tori (Diophantine: |ω·k| > γ|k|⁻τ) — survive as invariant tori
phase_2.fuse_element: KAM theorem: the union of surviving non-resonant tori plus measure-zero resonant set
phase_2.fuse_result: the perturbed phase space is recovered as a union of invariant tori (non-resonant, measure-full) plus a measure-zero chaotic set (resonant), reconstituting the original phase space structure

phase_3.void_description: 00 — the unperturbed integrable system H₀ before any perturbation is applied, no distinction between resonant and non-resonant tori yet exists
phase_3.true_description: 01 — a non-resonant torus satisfying the Diophantine condition, survives the perturbation, remains invariant under the perturbed flow
phase_3.false_description: 10 — a resonant torus where ω·k = 0 for some k, destroyed or chaotically perturbed, does not survive as an invariant torus
phase_3.both_description: 11 — the boundary layer near resonance where tori exist in superposition: for ε small enough, some resonant tori may survive (albedo stage), held without resolution until ε is specified

steps.domain_action: unperturbed integrable Hamiltonian H₀ with action variables I and angle variables θ, genesis of the system
the KAM torus invariant set — the boundary that contains all surviving tori under perturbation
apply perturbation H = H₀ + εH₁, forward morphism toward nearly-integrable regime
compose canonical transformations to construct action-angle variables for the perturbed system
the action variables I are self-referential invariants, they identify themselves under transformation
dichotomize phase space into resonant tori (ω·k = 0) and non-resonant tori (Diophantine condition)
evaluate non-resonant branch — Diophantine condition |ω·k| > γ|k|⁻τ is satisfied, affirmative
forward morphism on non-resonant branch — apply perturbation theory to show survival
compose near-identity canonical transformation to eliminate resonant terms
the action variables remain self-recognizing under the near-identity transformation
reconstitute the non-resonant branch — prove that these tori persist as invariant tori
evaluate resonant branch — resonance condition ω·k = 0 is met, negative evaluation
reverse morphism on resonant branch — descend into the chaotic regime, coagula of destroyed structure
boundary layer paradice — near resonance, tori exist in superposition of survival and destruction
record the measure-theoretic result — the set of destroyed tori has measure zero, permanent and irreversible
fuse both branches — the perturbed phase space is the union of surviving non-resonant tori (measure-full) and measure-zero chaotic set
reverse morphism — as ε → 0, recover the original unperturbed action variables and integrable structure
the action variables return to self-reference, the system recognizes itself in the limit
the KAM torus set closes and contains the final state — the theorem is complete

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Perturbation theory and Fourier analysis — translates the Hamiltonian H₀ + εH₁ into resonance conditions and near-identity transformations
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Poisson bracket {·,·} — components (resonant and non-resonant tori) communicate via canonical structure
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Action-angle variables (I, θ) — state is stored as action integrals and angle coordinates, retrieved via canonical transformations
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Perturbation parameter ε — determines the order and timing of resonance destruction; smaller ε preserves more tori
phase_5.alfs_store: The integrable Hamiltonian H₀ and its action-angle representation — the canonical reference and bootstrap store for the entire KAM structure

phase_6.cycle_cost: Perturbation energy ε — the cost per cycle is the magnitude of the perturbation H₁
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Integrable system with full set of invariant tori, zero entropy production, completely ordered phase space
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Nearly-integrable system with measure-full set of surviving non-resonant tori plus measure-zero chaotic set, entropy production localized to resonant regions
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the KAM theorem is entropy-conservative because the measure of destroyed tori is zero; the system remains nearly integrable and reversible, with chaos confined to a set of measure zero

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FFUSE → EVALF → AREV → ENGAGR → IFIX → FFUSE → AREV → IMSCRIB → TANCH
-- Class: Weinstein Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(12,3,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=19
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 10)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [8] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [9] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑚 → 𐑙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [10] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑖  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [11] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑙 → 𐑗  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [12] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑖 → 𐑳  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [13] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑗 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [14] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [15] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑭 → 𐑗  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [16] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑙 → 𐑠  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [17] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑗 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [18] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def weinstein_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (unperturbed integrable Hamiltonian H₀ with action variables I and angle varia...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the KAM torus invariant set — the boundary that contains all surviving tori u...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (apply perturbation H = H₀ + εH₁, forward morphism toward nearly-integrable re...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose canonical transformations to construct action-angle variables for the...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [4] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (the action variables I are self-referential invariants, they identify themsel...)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (dichotomize phase space into resonant tori (ω·k = 0) and non-resonant tori (Diophantine condition)) / FFUSE [10] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (4 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (evaluate non-resonant branch — Diophantine condition |ω·k| > γ|k|⁻τ is satisf...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (forward morphism on non-resonant branch — apply perturbation theory to show s...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose near-identity canonical transformation to eliminate resonant terms)
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (the action variables remain self-recognizing under the near-identity transfor...)
      -- F-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙))  -- F-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.F)
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑖)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (reconstitute the non-resonant branch — prove that these tori persist as invariant tori)
  (.arrow 𐑖 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [11] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (evaluate resonant branch — resonance condition ω·k = 0 is met, negative evalu...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑖 𐑳)  -- [12] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (reverse morphism on resonant branch — descend into the chaotic regime, coagul...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑗 𐑭)  -- [13] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (boundary layer paradice — near resonance, tori exist in superposition of surv...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑙)  -- [14] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the measure-theoretic result — the set of destroyed tori has measure z...)
  (.arrow 𐑙 𐑭 𐑗)  -- [15] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 | fuse μ — assembly mode (fuse both branches — the perturbed phase space is the union of surviving non-...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [16] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (reverse morphism — as ε → 0, recover the original unperturbed action variable...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑗 𐑡)  -- [17] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (the action variables return to self-reference, the system recognizes itself i...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [18] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the KAM torus set closes and contains the final state — the theorem is complete)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def weinstein_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (weinstein_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def weinstein_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (weinstein_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem weinstein_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
